rag_rules: Fix type mismatches by ensuring all expressions in AGREE contracts have consistent types (int, real, bool).
Resolve 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by checking that all identifiers are properly declared or visible.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and guard previous-value comparisons with an initialized flag.
Correct 'Couldn't resolve reference' errors by ensuring all referenced variables are declared in the same AGREE annex or are visible AADL features.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are properly scoped and declared before use.
Use consistent type annotations in AGREE expressions to avoid '<error>' type issues.
Apply initialized history patterns for temporal logic involving previous values.
Validate that all helper declarations and constants are compatible with the expected expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE requires explicit type declarations for constants and equations: const name : type = value; eq name : type = expression;
Use nested if-then-else for branching logic in AGREE contracts.
Guard pre() expressions with an initialized boolean flag to avoid undefined behavior.
Ensure binary expressions in AGREE use operands of matching types (int, real, bool).
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing undeclared or non-visible identifiers in AGREE contracts.
Do not mix incompatible types in binary expressions (e.g., int and <error>).
Do not assume that AADL feature names are automatically available in AGREE without proper declaration or scoping.
